EBS Pollock stock assessment

Scaffold based on Alaska Groundfish Stock Assessment Guidelines (July 2024)

Authors

Jim Ianelli

[Co-authors]

Published

April 24, 2026

Notes on this scaffold

This file is a Tier 3 stock assessment scaffold derived from the structure in:

  • Alaska Groundfish Stock Assessment Guidelines (AFSC, July 2024)

Use this as a starting template for either:

  • Operational Full assessments (complete detail), or
  • Operational Update assessments (abbreviated sections with references to prior full assessment).

Where useful, this template includes prompts for using the sporc workflow in this repo.

1 Introduction

1.1 September Plan Team products

[Describe preliminary work, bridging runs, and proposed model changes reviewed in September.]

1.2 November Plan Team products

[Describe final draft scope, products, and any constraints on post-submission changes.]

1.3 December Council products

[Describe final outputs, presentations, and SIS reporting status.]

1.4 Assessment product schedule

[State whether this is an operational full, update, or harvest projection year.]

1.5 Unforeseen circumstances

[Document material timeline or data/process disruptions, if any.]

2 Outline and format of stock assessments

[Optional short orientation text for reviewers.]

3 Assessment definitions

[State assessment type and rationale.]

  • Assessment type this year: [Operational Full / Operational Update / Harvest Projection]
  • Tier: 3
  • Base model carried forward from: [year/chapter]

4 Operational full and update guidelines

4.1 Title page and authorship

[Include required authorship/affiliation/disclaimer text as needed by current SAFE conventions.]

4.2 Executive summary

4.2.1 Summary of changes in assessment inputs

[Bullet list of data/model/process updates since last accepted assessment.]

4.2.2 Summary of results

[Concise summary of biomass, fishing mortality, reference points, OFL/ABC outcomes.]

4.2.3 Responses to SSC and Plan Team comments

4.2.3.1 General stock assessment comments

[Response table or bullets.]

4.2.3.2 Comments specific to this assessment

[Response table or bullets.]

4.3 Introduction

[Stock-specific context, geography, life history, fishery relevance.]

4.4 Fishery and management history

[Management timeline, major changes, current regulations, catch history context.]

4.5 Data

4.5.1 Fishery data

[Catch, composition, effort, age/length availability, quality checks, key caveats.]

4.6 Survey data

[Survey design, index generation, uncertainty, recent anomalies, inclusion/exclusion decisions.]

4.7 Other data

[Tagging, maturity, environmental covariates, or other sources if used.]

4.8 Analytic approach

[Describe model platform and configuration.]

4.8.1 SPORC implementation notes

  • Config file(s): config/base.yml, config/scenarios.yml
  • Input building: R/build_inputs.R and/or data-raw/build_ebs_pollock_data.R
  • Scenario runs: analysis/run_scenarios.R

4.8.2 Description of alternative models

[For full assessments: list alternatives, rationale, and selection logic.]

4.9 Age structured model results

4.9.1 Tables

[Insert required SAFE tables for Tier 3.]

4.9.2 Figures

[Insert required SAFE figures for Tier 3.]

4.9.3 Evaluation of model(s) and associated uncertainty

[Diagnostics + uncertainty interpretation.]

4.9.4 Sensitivity to model specification

[Parameter/process/data sensitivity cases and implications.]

4.9.5 Convergence status and criteria

[Convergence checks, gradients, Hessian, warnings, remedies.]

4.9.6 Likelihood profiles on key parameters

[Profiles and interpretation.]

4.9.7 Retrospective analysis (within model)

[Retrospective setup/results and interpretation.]

4.9.8 Historical retrospectives (between models)

[Cross-version retrospective comparisons, if applicable.]

4.10 Projections and harvest recommendations

4.10.1 Amendment 56 reference points

[Specify reference points used.]

4.10.2 Specification of OFL and maximum permissible ABC

[Derivation and final values.]

4.10.3 Standard harvest scenarios and projection methodology

[Projection assumptions, scenarios, and implementation details.]

4.11 Risk table and ABC recommendation

[Risk table summary and recommended ABC rationale.]

4.12 Area allocation of ABC

[Allocation method and resulting values.]

4.13 Status determination

[Overfishing/overfished status determination text.]

4.14 F_limit

[If applicable under current guidance.]

4.15 Ecosystem considerations

[Concise ecosystem context relevant to management advice.]

4.16 Ecosystem effects on the stock

[Effects of ecosystem/state shifts on stock dynamics and uncertainty.]

4.17 Fishery effects on the ecosystem

[Fishery/ecosystem interactions and management implications.]

4.18 Data gaps and research priorities

[Prioritized list for next cycle(s).]

4.19 Acknowledgements

[Contributors, reviewers, data providers, etc.]

4.20 Literature cited

[Automatically generated from bibliography.]

4.21 Auxiliary files

[List model/data/output artifacts and repository locations.]

4.22 Tables

[Consolidated table section if required by chapter formatting.]

4.23 Figures

[Consolidated figure section if required by chapter formatting.]

4.24 Appendices and working papers

[Include supplemental methods, diagnostics, and additional analyses.]
